title: fix(docs): Fix glossary FIXME tags
body:
This PR addresses two doc-related issues identified during local builds:

- Fix a misnamed Sphinx extension that breaks local doc builds. The doc/conf.py currently lists the extension 'sphinx-prompt', which is not the correct import name for Sphinx. It should be 'sphinx_prompt'.
- Clean up an inline FIXME tag in the glossary. The glossary entry for meta-estimators contains a FIXME note about Pipeline and FeatureUnion not cloning contained estimators during fitting. Replace this with a neutral, up-to-date note to avoid stale or confusing TODOs in the glossary.

These changes are purely documentation/configuration changes and do not modify any runtime logic.

step_plan.summary: This PR fixes a misnamed Sphinx extension to restore local doc builds and cleans up a glossary FIXME tag for clarity.

steps.operation: Update Sphinx extension name and glossary entry
steps.reason: Rename the Sphinx extension from 'sphinx-prompt' to 'sphinx_prompt' to match the actual import name and prevent a ModuleNotFoundError during local doc builds. Additionally, replace the FIXME tag in doc/glossary.rst within the meta-estimator entry with a neutral, up-to-date note to improve glossary clarity and avoid stale TODOs.
steps.side_effects: Enables local documentation builds (e.g., make html) without extension import errors; glossary now contains a clear, non-declarative note. May require the environment to have the sphinx_prompt package installed. No runtime code changes; affects docs rendering and developer guidance. Ensure a follow-up doc build verifies the fix and confirm no remaining FIXME markers in glossary sections.
